title: Roulinia longifolia
taxonomy: Roulinia longifolia belongs to the family Fabaceae and is a part of the genus Roulinia. It is a species of flowering plant native to South America. Within the family Fabaceae, Roulinia longifolia is classified as a legume. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but Fabaceae is widely accepted. The plant's classification is primarily based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships with other species.

The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help in treating wounds and infections. Its leaves and bark are used to make teas and infusions that are consumed to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Additionally, Roulinia longifolia is thought to have antiseptic properties, which can aid in the treatment of skin conditions and ulcers. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of this plant.
